# UC-22.2.41 · NIS2 Management Body Cybersecurity Training Evidence

## Description

Correlates the official management-body roster against `lms:completion` rows in `index=training` and surfaces people who never completed the mandated NIS2 governance curriculum or whose `completion_date` is more than 365 days old.

## Value

Produces repeatable training-gap evidence for board and supervisory conversations without manually reconciling spreadsheets each quarter, so legal and people teams can intervene before governance reviews or competent-authority questions materialise.

## Implementation

(1) Keep `nis2_management_roster.csv` aligned with corporate registry filings and `board_member` flags. (2) Publish stable `course_code` values from the LMS for NIS2 leadership modules. (3) Grant search-only RBAC (`srchIndexesAllowed` includes `training`) to HR compliance analysts; restrict roster lookup updates to `lookup_editor` service accounts. (4) Route alert output to the `audit_evidence` index or GRC tooling. (5) Pair with UC-22.2.20 for governance dashboards.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud 9.x search head with RBAC role granting `srchIndexesAllowed=training` plus read access to `nis2_management_roster.csv` under `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/search/lookups/` (or equivalent KV store collection).
• Ingest path A: HTTP Event Collector token configured under **Settings → Data inputs → HTTP Event Collector**, writing JSON lines with `sourcetype=lms:completion` into `index=training`. Ingest path B: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 ([Splunkbase 4055](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4055)) if the LMS SSO trusts Entra ID and you already centralise Azure AD sign-in risk there — still map LMS completion webhooks into `lms:completion` via a heavy forwarder.
• LMS export or API must populate `person_id`, `course_code`, `course_name`, `completion_date`, `score`, `role`, and `department` before events hit `_indextime`; HR validates `nis2_management_roster` columns weekly.
• Baseline expectation: each board or executive member completes `NIS2_EXEC` or `CYBER-BOARD-101` at least annually — the SPL uses a **730-day** lookback window to catch long leaves without truncating past proof.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Create `props.conf` (app context, e.g. `training_lms/local/props.conf`) for `lms:completion` with `TIME_PREFIX`/`TIME_FORMAT` aligned to `completion_date`, and add `FIELDALIAS-` stanzas if the LMS emits a different employee key than `person_id`. For HEC, set `indexes=training` on the token stanza in `inputs.conf` Global Settings.

Deployment checklist: (1) Verify `btool` merge — `splunk btool props list lms:completion --debug`. (2) Confirm **30-day** retention on `index=training` meets your policy; extend if HR needs historical proof. (3) Document the mapping from Workday/HCM IDs to `person_id` to avoid orphan joins.

Step 2 — Create the search
Save as `UC-22.2.41 — NIS2 management training gaps` (Settings → Searches, reports, and alerts). Paste the SPL from the `spl` field, then schedule weekly with lookback `-730d` embedded in the subsearch.

Understanding this SPL: `inputlookup` loads every in-scope leader. `join type=left` preserves roster rows even when no LMS event exists (the gap you must prove). `stats latest(completion_date)` collapses replays. `gap_reason` labels either a missing course or a completion older than 365 days, and `evidence_id` holds a stable hash for export bundles.

Optional alert action: webhook to ServiceNow GRC with `evidence_id` in the payload.

Step 3 — Validate
• Run against a known trained chair: the row must disappear unless you force `days_since>365` in a dev index. • Compare counts to the LMS **Completion report** UI for the same fiscal week — variance should be zero. • Spot-check `person_id` joins: `| inputlookup nis2_management_roster.csv | stats dc(person_id)` must equal HR’s headcount file. • permissions test: log in as `hr_compliance_reader` and confirm `rest /services/authentication/users/` shows only the delegated role — no `admin`.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard row 1: single value `open_gaps`. Row 2: drillable table sorted by `board_member desc`. Row 3: trend of `missing_course` counts after each AGM. Attach runbook link describing how Legal uses the export. Archive weekly CSV snapshots to immutable storage and record SHA-256 beside each `evidence_id`.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Empty main search after upgrade** — Confirm token still lists `index=training` in **Settings → Tokens** and forwarders were not switched to a dev index; rerun `index=training sourcetype=lms:completion | head 5`.
• **All executives suddenly stale** — LMS changed `course_code`; update the `where` clause in the subsearch and publish change record.
• **Join drops non-matching keys** — Remove accidental inner join; ensure `rename` after `stats` keeps `roster_person` aligned.
• **Performance timeouts** — Narrow earliest to **365d** in the subsearch if indexers struggle, but document the shorter evidence window to auditors.
• **PII exposure** — Strip `name` from email templates; send only `person_id` externally and resolve names inside Splunk.

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_management_roster.csv
| eval roster_person=person_id
| join type=left roster_person [
    search index=training sourcetype=lms:completion (course_code="NIS2_EXEC" OR course_code="CYBER-BOARD-101") earliest=-730d
    | stats latest(completion_date) as last_completion latest(score) as last_score by person_id
    | rename person_id as roster_person
]
| eval last_completion_epoch=if(isnotnull(last_completion), strptime(last_completion,"%Y-%m-%d"), null())
| eval days_since=if(isnotnull(last_completion_epoch), round((now()-last_completion_epoch)/86400,0), null())
| eval gap_reason=case(isnull(last_completion),"missing_course",days_since>365,"stale_over_365d",true(),null())
| where isnotnull(gap_reason)
| eval evidence_id=md5(roster_person . "|NIS2_MGMT_TRAIN|" . if(isnotnull(last_completion), last_completion, "none"))
| table person_id, name, role, board_member, last_completion, last_score, days_since, gap_reason, evidence_id
```

## Visualization

Table of roster rows with `gap_reason`, `days_since`, and `evidence_id`; single value for count of `stale_over_365d`; bar chart by `role`; timeline of `last_completion` per `person_id`.
